description: Replace synergyWith/batchWith/conflictsWith relationships with runtime file-overlap detection from touchesFiles. Add parallel task execution where non-conflicting tasks run simultaneously in separate git worktrees using a wave-based model (max 3 slots).

criteria: Rust: No warnings in `cargo check` output
Rust: No warnings in `cargo clippy` output
Rust: All tests pass with `cargo test`
No breaking changes to existing APIs unless explicitly required
--parallel 1 (default) produces identical behavior to current sequential execution
Old PRD JSON with synergyWith/batchWith/conflictsWith parses without error
globalAcceptanceCriteria.description: These criteria apply to ALL implementation tasks

priorityPhilosophy.description: Hierarchy of what matters most when implementing tasks
hierarchy: 1. PLAN — Anticipate edge cases before coding
2. PHASE 2 FOUNDATION — ~1 day now to save ~2+ weeks later (1:10+ ratio); we are pre-launch, foundations compound
3. FUNCTIONING CODE — Pragmatic, reliable, wired in per plan
4. CORRECTNESS — Compiles, type-checks, scoped tests pass deterministically
5. CODE QUALITY — Clean code, qualityDimensions satisfied, no warnings
principles: Quality dimensions explicit — qualityDimensions on every task tells you what 'good' looks like
Phase 2 foundation — prefer solutions that lay strong post-launch foundations (1:10+ savings ratio)
Edge cases = test cases — every known edge case must have a corresponding test
Scoped per-iteration tests, full suite at milestones — milestones must leave the trunk green including pre-existing failures
Ship working code with tests to prove it; handle Option/Result explicitly; avoid unwrap() in production

prohibitedOutcomes: Tests that only assert 'no crash' or check type without verifying content
Tests that mirror implementation internals (break when refactoring)
Abstractions with only one concrete use
Error messages that don't identify what went wrong
Catch-all error handlers that swallow context
Two tasks with overlapping touchesFiles in the same parallel group
Sharing IterationContext across threads — each slot gets its own minimal state
Using git worktree add on a branch already checked out — git forbids this

priorityGuidelines.medium: 21-50: Fix in current phase if time permits

acceptanceCriteria: Test: select_parallel_group with 2 tasks sharing a file returns group of 1
Test: select_parallel_group with 2 disjoint-file tasks returns group of 2
Test: task with empty touchesFiles can always parallelize
Test: group size respects max_slots parameter
Test: group is ordered by score (highest first)
Test: single eligible task returns group of 1
Test: all eligible tasks sharing one file → sequential, group of 1
Known-bad discriminator: test that returns group>1 only when files are truly disjoint (would fail if conflict check skipped)
Test file compiles (tests may be #[ignore] until FEAT tasks implement)
userStories.description: Write TDD tests for select_parallel_group() function and simplified 2-dimension scoring (priority_score + file_score only). Tests define the contract before implementation.
edgeCases: All eligible tasks share the same file: parallel group degenerates to 1 task
Task has empty touchesFiles: can always parallelize — empty set has no overlap with any set
Single eligible task: returns group of 1 regardless of max_slots
max_slots=1: always returns exactly 1 task (sequential fallback)

failureModes.expectedBehavior: Compilation error or #[ignore] — tests define the contract

invariants: No two tasks in a parallel group share any file path in touchesFiles
Group contains only dependency-eligible tasks (all dependsOn satisfied)
userStories.notes: Learning [522]: Incremental field addition to IterationResult — follow same pattern for new structs. The existing get_all_task_files() (selection.rs:358) returns HashMap<String, Vec<String>> — use this to build the inverted file index for conflict detection.
qualityDimensions: File conflict detection must be conservative: any shared file = conflict
Tasks with empty touchesFiles have no conflicts (empty set overlap is empty)
Group selection is greedy by score order — highest priority first
No additional DB queries beyond what select_next_task already does
userStories.title: Initial tests for parallel group selection and simplified scoring

Test: slots 1-2 get worktrees at {repo}-worktrees/{branch}-slot-{N}/
Test: ephemeral branches named {branch}-slot-{N}
Test: merge_slot_branches merges disjoint changes back to main branch

Test: git worktree add on same branch fails — ephemeral branches required
Test file compiles (tests may be #[ignore] until FEAT tasks implement)
userStories.description: Write TDD tests for per-slot worktree management with ephemeral branches. Tests verify that git's branch exclusivity constraint is respected.
edgeCases: git worktree add on already-checked-out branch: must use ephemeral branch, not same branch
Merge with disjoint file changes: should always succeed (no conflicts)
Cleanup when worktree doesn't exist: should not error

failureModes.cause: git worktree add refuses same branch

invariants: Each slot worktree is on a unique branch
Slot 0 is always the main branch worktree
After merge_slot_branches, main branch contains all slots' commits
userStories.notes: Learning [791]: Integration test pattern for worktree chaining — setup_git_repo_with_file for temp repo. Learning [1329]: git merge --no-commit --no-ff safely tests merges. CRITICAL: git worktrees cannot share a branch — this is the core constraint these tests verify.
qualityDimensions: Tests must use real git repos (tempdir), not mocks
Verify git's branch exclusivity constraint is handled correctly
Merge tests must use disjoint files to prove conflict-free merge

userStories.description: Add select_parallel_group() to selection.rs that selects up to N non-conflicting tasks by building an inverted file index and greedily selecting tasks in score order.
userStories.notes: get_all_task_files() (selection.rs:358) already returns HashMap<String, Vec<String>>. Build inverted index: HashMap<&str, HashSet<usize>> mapping file_path → indices of already-selected tasks. O(tasks * avg_files) complexity, sub-10ms for 200 tasks. Data contract: get_all_task_files returns task_id→Vec<file_path>; invert to file_path→Set<selected_indices>.
qualityDimensions: File conflict detection must be conservative: any shared file = conflict

Learning [1329]: git merge --no-commit --no-ff safely tests merges. Learning [311]: Worktree branch points can trail behind main.

acceptanceCriteria: SlotContext struct: slot_index, working_root, task, last_activity_epoch
SlotResult struct: slot_index, iteration_result
WaveResult struct: outcomes Vec<SlotResult>, wave_duration
run_slot_iteration() does NOT take &mut IterationContext

run_slot_iteration() builds prompt, spawns Claude in slot worktree, parses output
run_slot_iteration() checks signal_flag.is_signaled()
run_parallel_wave() claims all tasks sequentially before spawning threads
run_parallel_wave() spawns std::thread::spawn per slot
run_parallel_wave() waits via JoinHandle::join() (wave model)
run_parallel_wave() handles thread panics gracefully (no unwrap on join)
userStories.description: Create the parallel wave execution engine: SlotContext, run_slot_iteration (simplified run_iteration without shared IterationContext), and run_parallel_wave (thread spawning + join).
userStories.notes: IterationContext (engine.rs:176) is NOT thread-safe — do NOT share across threads. Each slot gets its own minimal state. spawn_claude already accepts working_dir (claude.rs SpawnOpts). Each spawn_claude creates its own watchdog thread — timeouts are per-subprocess. SignalFlag is Arc<AtomicBool> — thread-safe sharing. run_loop is async fn but blocking thread::join is fine (existing path already blocks on child.wait). Learning [1601]: Update all spawn_claude callers when adding new arg. Learning [135]: Use multi-iteration sequences to test state machine transitions.
qualityDimensions: IterationContext never shared across threads
Each slot opens its own DB connection (WAL supports concurrent access)
Thread panics caught via JoinHandle::join().is_err()
Per-slot independent watchdog timeouts
userStories.title: Wave-based parallel execution engine

CrashTracker policy: all slots crash → increment; any succeeds → reset
Progress logging happens sequentially on main thread after wave completes
log_iteration gains slot: Option<usize> parameter
One wave = one iteration toward max_iterations budget
Reorder hints from parallel slots queued for next wave
userStories.description: Wire parallel wave execution into run_loop()'s main iteration loop. Add branching: sequential path for parallel=1, wave path for parallel>1. Add slot-aware progress logging.
userStories.notes: engine.rs: main loop at line 1713. Insert branching on config.parallel_slots. After wave: merge results from WaveResult into IterationContext (extend last_files, update crash_tracker). Progress logging (progress.rs:38): add slot parameter, log sequentially after wave (not during). Learning [1005]: 9 consecutive retries on same blocked task wastes iterations — crash policy prevents this in parallel mode too.
qualityDimensions: Sequential path (parallel=1) completely unchanged
Progress logged sequentially after wave, not during
CrashTracker: all-crash → increment, any-success → reset
Main thread handles all post-wave state merging
userStories.title: Wire parallel wave into run_loop main iteration loop

acceptanceCriteria: No unwrap()/expect() on branch_name in the parallel dispatch arm
Behavior preserved when branch_name is Some (the only real case)

userStories.description: From CODE-REVIEW-2: src/loop_engine/engine.rs:2835 uses `branch_name.as_deref().expect("parallel_active implies branch")`. The invariant holds (Step 9.5 only sets parallel_active=true when branch is Some) but the global AC says 'No unwrap() in production code paths'. expect() panics on invariant violation, defeating the spirit of the rule. Refactor to a `let Some(branch) = branch_name.as_deref() else { /* warn + skip wave dispatch */ }` pattern so a programming error degrades to sequential rather than crashing.
userStories.title: Replace expect() in parallel run_loop branch with graceful pattern

acceptanceCriteria: Inter-wave signal returns exit code 130, matching step 0/step 13
Inter-wave .stop file path (if reachable here) still returns 0
Existing wave tests still pass
Add a regression test asserting 130 is returned when signal_flag is set during inter-wave delay
userStories.description: From CODE-REVIEW-2: in src/loop_engine/engine.rs:1217-1236, when the signal flag is set during the inter-wave sleep poll, the wave returns terminal Some((0, "stop signal", None)). All other signal paths in run_wave_iteration return (130, "signal received", None) — see step 0 (line 793) and step 13 (line 1174). The mismatched exit code muddies operator triage of why the loop stopped (clean stop file vs. SIGINT) and is inconsistent with sequential run_iteration semantics. Branch on `signal_flag.is_signaled()` after the loop exits and return 130 in that case.
userStories.title: Use exit code 130 (not 0) when SIGINT/SIGTERM hits during inter-wave delay

cargo clippy -- -D warnings passes
No other tests regress
userStories.description: Pre-existing bug surfaced by MILESTONE-3 gate: commit 456834f wrapped edit_learning in an unchecked_transaction. apply_proposals_in_transaction in src/commands/curate/enrich.rs also opens an unchecked_transaction and calls edit_learning inside, causing SQLite error: cannot start a transaction within a transaction. Three integration tests fail: test_curate_enrich_populates_metadata, test_curate_enrich_skips_retired_learnings, test_curate_enrich_idempotent_second_run_zero_candidates.
userStories.title: Fix nested-transaction bug in curate enrich
